paragraphs.text: (WHOSE LIFE, 1S BRIEFLY DESCRIBED) NAMED CONON WHEN GOD WORKED A MIRACLE FOR HIM

» In the community of our holy father Theodosios the Archimandrite* One of the fathers told me that a military standard-bearer® had told there was an elder named Conon, a naive of Cilicia. This is the rule him this: of life which he maintained for thirty-five years: he partook of bread We were in a battle with the Mauritanians in the African provinces; and water once a week, he worked unceasingly and he nover went the barbarians defeated us and put us to flight. They pursued us and out of the church. many of us were slain. One of the barbarians caught up with me and raised his spear to strike at me. When I saw this, I began 23, THE LIFE OF THEODOULOS THE MONK calling on God. "Lord God’, I said; “You who appeared to your servant Thecla® and delivered her {rom impious hands: deliver me We saw another elder in that same monastery, a former soldier* from this calamity and save me from this bitter death. Then I wil) named Theodoulos who fasted every day, never wore shoes and go and lead a life of solitude® in the desert’. When I turned rouad, never slept lying down.” there was nol a barbarian in sight, I came straight away to this Lavra of Kopratha* and, by the grace of God, I have lived thirty years in this cave.
